steps.dialogue: Welcome back! Last time you learned about unit fractions - when we select exactly one part from equal parts.

But you also showed that you understood why unit fractions have a 1 on top. You discovered the pattern where bigger denominators mean smaller pieces. When you saw one-eighth, you KNEW it would be tiny compared to one-half, not because I told you, but because you understood the logic! That was great work.
The way you found patterns in the last lesson is exactly how math experts think. And it will help you solve new problems and get even stronger in math.
Today, we'll keep exploring unit fractions.
What you'll learn today will help your understanding of fractions grow even stronger.
